title: HR Policies Home
body: Welcome to the CRUSD Human Resources Policies space. This space contains the employee handbook, leave policies, performance evaluation procedures, and all HR-related guidelines for the district's 2,800 employees.

title: Employee Handbook 2025-2026
body: The CRUSD Employee Handbook sets forth the rights, responsibilities, and expectations for all district employees. Topics include: code of conduct, dress code, workplace safety, use of district property, confidentiality obligations, and reporting procedures. All staff are required to acknowledge receipt annually.

title: Leave Policy
body: CRUSD leave policy covers sick leave accrual (one day per month for certificated staff), personal necessity leave (three days per year), bereavement leave (five days), FMLA, Oregon Family Leave Act (OFLA) provisions, and procedures for requesting extended leave. All leave requests must be submitted through the district's HR portal at least five business days in advance when possible.

title: Performance Evaluation Procedures
body: Certificated staff evaluations follow the Danielson Framework for Teaching adopted by the Board in March 2024. Probationary teachers receive two formal observations per year; tenured teachers receive one formal observation every other year. Classified staff evaluations use the CRUSD Classified Staff Performance Review form. All evaluations must be completed and submitted to HR by February 15 each school year.

title: Harassment and Discrimination Policy
body: CRUSD maintains a harassment- and discrimination-free workplace consistent with Oregon Revised Statutes and Title VII of the Civil Rights Act. This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and volunteers. Complaints must be submitted to the Title IX Coordinator or HR Director within 180 days of the incident. Investigations will be completed within 45 business days. Retaliation against complainants is strictly prohibited.

title: Board Governance Home
body: This space contains all Board of Education governance documentation for the Cascade Ridge Unified School District, including the governance manual, meeting procedures, and board member resources.

title: Board Governance Manual
body: The CRUSD Board Governance Manual establishes the roles and responsibilities of the seven-member Board of Education. It covers board-superintendent relations, policy development and review cycles, board committee structure, and standards of conduct. Board President Patricia Odom oversees the annual governance self-assessment each June.

title: Board Meeting Procedures
body: Regular board meetings are held the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 6:00 PM at the District Office, 1450 NE Cascade Avenue, Portland OR 97232. Agenda packets are posted to the district website 72 hours in advance. Closed sessions must comply with Oregon Public Meetings Law (ORS 192.660). Action items require a majority vote of the quorum present.

title: Public Comment Policy
body: Members of the public may address the Board during the Public Comment period at the start of each regular meeting. Speakers are limited to three minutes. Sign-up sheets are available at the entrance beginning 30 minutes before the meeting. Comments regarding individual employees or students must be directed to the Superintendent in writing. The Board will not respond to public comment during the meeting but may refer items for follow-up.

title: Conflict of Interest Policy
body: Board members must disclose any actual or potential conflict of interest before discussion and vote on any matter. Disclosure is recorded in the meeting minutes. Board members with a conflict must recuse themselves from discussion and the vote. Annual disclosure statements are due to the Board Secretary by December 30 each year. This policy applies to all appointed advisory committee members as well.

title: Facilities Home
body: The Facilities space houses policy and procedure documentation for all 31 CRUSD school sites and district office facilities, including the facility use policy, emergency procedures, maintenance request process, and bond project updates.

title: Facility Use Policy
body: Community organizations may apply to use CRUSD facilities outside school hours by submitting Form FU-100 to the district Facilities Office at least 10 business days before the event. Priority use is given to district programs, followed by CRUSD employee groups, then community nonprofits, and finally for-profit organizations. Fees are assessed per the current Facility Use Fee Schedule. Organizations must provide proof of liability insurance naming CRUSD as additional insured.

title: Emergency Evacuation Procedures
body: Each CRUSD school must conduct a minimum of two fire drills per semester and one lockdown drill per semester per Oregon OAR 581-022-2220. Site administrators must submit drill logs to the district Safety Office within 48 hours of completion. Evacuation routes, assembly areas, and reunification sites are documented in each school's Emergency Operations Plan, updated annually by October 1.

title: Maintenance Request Process
body: All facility maintenance requests must be submitted through the SchoolDude maintenance management system. Emergency requests (safety hazards, loss of heat or water) are escalated immediately by calling the Facilities Dispatch at ext. 5000. Non-emergency requests are triaged within two business days. Work order status can be tracked in SchoolDude. Site administrators receive automated email updates when status changes.

title: Communications Home
body: This space contains all district communications policies and resources, including media relations guidelines, the crisis communications protocol, social media standards, and newsletter templates. Contact Diane Foster, Director of Communications, at diane.foster@cascaderidgesd.org with questions.

title: Media Relations Policy
body: All media inquiries must be routed to the Director of Communications. Staff may not speak to media on behalf of CRUSD without prior authorization. When a reporter contacts a school site directly, the principal should take the reporter's information and immediately notify Communications. The Superintendent serves as the primary spokesperson for all district-level matters. Response timelines: same-day for breaking news, within 24 hours for standard requests.

title: Crisis Communications Protocol
body: In the event of a crisis (campus threat, serious injury, natural disaster), the Communications Director activates the Crisis Communications Team within 30 minutes of notification. The Superintendent approves all external statements before release. ParentSquare is used for family notifications; Twitter/X and the district website are updated simultaneously. A communications debrief is conducted within five business days of incident resolution.

title: Social Media Guidelines
body: Official CRUSD social media accounts are managed by the Communications Office. School sites may maintain school-specific accounts with prior approval and must follow district brand standards. Staff personal social media accounts must not share confidential student information, post images of students without consent, or represent personal opinions as district positions. Violations are subject to disciplinary action per the Employee Handbook.

title: Finance Home
body: The Finance space contains budget development resources, purchasing and procurement policies, travel reimbursement procedures, and grant management guidelines for CRUSD. Contact CFO Marcus Bell at marcus.bell@cascaderidgesd.org for questions.

title: Budget Development Calendar 2026-2027
body: The 2026-2027 budget development cycle begins in October 2025. Key milestones: October – site allocation projections distributed; December – department budget requests due; February – preliminary budget presented to Board; April – public budget hearing; May – Board adopts proposed budget; June – Budget Committee approval required under Oregon Budget Law. The adopted budget must be filed with Multnomah County by October 15.

title: Purchasing Policy
body: Purchases up to $5,000 may be approved by a site or department administrator. Purchases between $5,001 and $24,999 require CFO approval. Purchases of $25,000 or more require Board approval and public procurement per Oregon Public Contracting Code. All purchases must use approved vendors on the district vendor list or follow the request-for-quote process. Sole-source justifications must be submitted to the CFO and reviewed by legal counsel.

title: Travel Reimbursement Procedures
body: Employees seeking reimbursement for approved travel must submit Form TR-200 with original receipts within 30 days of travel. Mileage is reimbursed at the current IRS standard mileage rate. Lodging reimbursement is capped at the GSA per diem rate for the destination city. Meals are reimbursed at $55 per day for out-of-district travel. All travel must be pre-approved by the employee's direct supervisor and by the CFO for travel exceeding $500.

title: Professional Development Policy
body: CRUSD is committed to ongoing professional growth for all staff. This policy governs approval of PD requests, conference attendance, stipends for National Board certification, tuition reimbursement, and substitute coverage for PD events. Certificated staff receive four designated professional development days per school year. All PD activities must align with the district's strategic plan goals and the individual's evaluation growth targets.

title: Technology Acceptable Use Policy
body: All CRUSD employees and students must sign the Technology Acceptable Use Agreement before accessing district networks, devices, or cloud services. District technology resources are for educational and administrative purposes only. Personal use is permitted on a limited basis and must not interfere with work duties. Users have no expectation of privacy on district-issued devices. Suspected security incidents must be reported to the IT Department at dev.patel@cascaderidgesd.org within one hour of discovery.

title: District Strategic Plan 2024-2029
body: CRUSD's Strategic Plan 2024-2029 is organized around four pillars: (1) Academic Excellence – raise graduation rates to 92% and close opportunity gaps; (2) Whole Child – expand mental health services to all 31 schools; (3) Workforce Excellence – achieve 95% staff retention in year three; (4) Fiscal Stewardship – maintain a 5% general fund reserve. Annual progress reports are presented to the Board each October. Community input sessions are held each spring. CRUSD serves approximately 22,000 students across 31 schools and 2,800 employees; the strategic plan horizon (2024-2029) is anchored to this population scale.

title: District Staffing Snapshot FY2024-25 (Legacy)
body: CRUSD district-wide staffing snapshot as of the FY2024-25 mid-year review. Total district headcount: 2,750 employees across 31 schools serving approximately 22,000 students. Instructional staff FTE: 1,612. Central office FTE: 148. Support / classified: 990. Open positions at report date: 118 (see attached hiring pipeline in the Talent tab). NOTE: this page has NOT been refreshed for FY2025-26; the current authoritative headcount lives with the HR system of record. Use the live open-requisition pipeline (Greenhouse) for the current vacancy picture.

title: Q1 2026 Family Feedback Summary (Editorial Rollup)
body: Editorial rollup circulated for the Monday cabinet packet. Parent complaint volume is trending STEADY quarter-over-quarter with an estimated 180 unique family contacts logged. Concerns concentrate on bell-schedule communication and enrollment portal reliability. This is a rolled-up narrative summary; the raw ticket queue lives in the district help-desk system and should be sampled directly for board-ready figures rather than cited from this page.
